title: Sisymbrium lasiocarpum
taxonomy: Sisymbrium lasiocarpum is classified within the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This family is part of the larger order Brassicales, which includes a wide variety of flowering plants. The family Brassicaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, including Brassicoideae and Brassiceae. The species Sisymbrium lasiocarpum is also known as hairy-fruited rockcress, and is native to North America. Its classification within the plant kingdom is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Rosids.

intro: Sisymbrium lasiocarpum, also known as prickly mustard or hairy-fruited rock-cress, is a biennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Sisymbrium lasiocarpum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Sisymbrium lasiocarpum is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
